title: P4 — LLM layer (thin abstraction, fat clients)
description: LLMClient is a deliberately THIN interface; most logic lives inside each client. Do NOT over-abstract. Build the 4 clients one at a time (one sub-bead each, end-to-end, correct + performant for that API): OpenAI/OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic Messages, Gemini interactions, OpenAI Responses. Plus in-repo live-test scripts under scripts/live/ (NOT CI) using a GitHub environment named 'llm'. The minimal shared interface is extracted LAST from what clients actually share. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P2 — Types & settings models
description: Transpile settings models and profiles. Pure-data; validates the zod approach at scale. Serialization round-trip tests. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P6 — Context, condenser, skills
description: Transpile context-window management, condensation, agent context, and skill discovery/validation. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P9 — Packaging, examples, docs, release 0.1.0
description: Finalize packaging (exports, files), write examples and docs, cut release 0.1.0. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P3 — Tool abstraction + registry
description: Transpile the tool module: base Tool, tool registry, JSON-schema generation via zod v4 z.toJSONSchema() (replaces pydantic model_json_schema()). Then one concrete tool end-to-end as a vertical slice. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P8 — Concrete tools (openhands-tools equivalent)
description: Transpile the concrete tools: terminal, file editor, browser, grep/glob, task tracker, etc. May become a separate package @smolpaws/openhands-tools later. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P7 — Surrounding subsystems: security, hooks, critic, subagent, git, mcp
description: Transpile the surrounding subsystems: security (confirmation/risk/analyzer), hooks, critic, subagent/delegation, git integration, mcp client. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: Plan: transpile Python OpenHands agent-sdk to idiomatic TypeScript
description:
# Plan: transpile the Python OpenHands agent-sdk to idiomatic TypeScript

## Objective

Produce `@smolpaws/openhands-agent`: a fresh, idiomatic TypeScript implementation of the
OpenHands Python `agent-sdk` (local source: `~/repos/agent-sdk`, upstream
`OpenHands/software-agent-sdk`). We transpile *anew* — we do **not** copy the outdated TS
attempt in `oh-tab/packages/agent-sdk`. That older code is reference-only (tooling, tests).

## Source scope (Python, local `~/repos/agent-sdk`)

Core package `openhands-sdk/openhands/sdk` (~59k LOC, 93 files use pydantic). Modules by size:

| Module | Files | ~LOC | Notes |
|--------|-------|------|-------|
| llm | 40 | 10364 | LiteLLM-backed; biggest + hardest. Provider abstraction, routing, caching |
| conversation | 29 | 8378 | Local + Remote conversation, state, event loop |
| agent | 9 | 7685 | The agent loop / step logic |
| context | 25 | 3301 | Condenser, skills context, agent context |
| settings | 5 | 3127 | Settings models |
| skills | 9 | 2586 | Skill discovery/validation |
| workspace | 10 | 2528 | Local/Remote/Apple workspace |
| tool | 11 | 2360 | Tool base + registry |
| security | 15 | 2084 | Confirmation, risk, analyzer |
| utils | 16 | 2004 | Shared helpers |
| event | 19 | 1923 | Event model hierarchy |
| hooks | 6 | 1669 | Lifecycle hooks |
| plugin | 7 | 1471 | Plugin system |
| critic | 12 | 1446 | Critic models |
| git | 6 | 1355 | Git integration |
| profiles | 5 | 1267 | LLM profiles |
| subagent | 4 | 1011 | Delegation |
| extensions | 8 | 988 | |
| mcp | 6 | 750 | MCP client |
| marketplace | 4 | 649 | |
| observability | 3 | 447 | |
| io | 5 | 431 | |
| logger | 3 | 330 | |
| testing | 2 | 339 | test helpers |
| secret | ? | 155 | secret handling |

Plus `openhands-tools` (~16k LOC): the concrete tools (terminal, file editor, browser, etc.).
And `openhands-agent-server` (separate concern — out of scope for the SDK package, may be a
later sibling package).

## Principles

1. **Idiomatic TS, not literal port.** Respect the architecture (module boundaries, the
   event/conversation/agent separation, tool abstraction) but use TS idioms: discriminated
   unions over class hierarchies where natural, `readonly`, narrow types, no Python-isms.
2. **Type enforcement is non-negotiable.** `strict` + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` +
   `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` + `verbatimModuleSyntax`. `no-explicit-any` is an error.
3. **Runtime validation = zod.** This answers the "z…" question: **zod** (+ zod-to-json-schema)
   is the pydantic equivalent and is already the choice in `oh-tab`. Pydantic `BaseModel`
   classes become zod schemas with inferred types (`z.infer`). zod-to-json-schema covers the
   places Python uses `model_json_schema()` (tool schemas, settings export).
   - DECISION TO CONFIRM: zod v3 (matches oh-tab) vs zod v4. Default to v3 for parity.
4. **No code copy.** Read Python for behavior, write TS fresh. Port tests conceptually too.
5. **Tooling parity with oh-tab** unless justified: tsup (ESM+CJS), vitest, eslint
   type-checked, tsc strict, target ES2022. (Scaffolding already laid down to match.)
6. **Wire-protocol compatibility.** The TS types must serialize to the same JSON the Python
   SDK and agent-server expect. This is the correctness anchor — every model gets a
   round-trip serialization test against Python-produced fixtures where possible.

## Phased roadmap (each phase = its own bead, dependency-chained)

- **P0 — Scaffolding & conventions** (this repo): package.json, tsconfig, tsup, vitest,
  eslint, CI. Decide zod version. Write CONTRIBUTING with the transpilation conventions
  (pydantic→zod patterns, naming, error model). DONE-ish; finalize conventions doc.
- **P1 — Foundations: utils, logger, io, event model.** Low-dependency leaf modules first.
  Establishes the zod patterns and the event discriminated-union shape everything builds on.
- **P2 — Types & settings.** Settings models, profiles. Pure-data, validates the zod approach
  at scale. Serialization round-trip tests.
- **P3 — Tool abstraction + tool registry.** The `tool` module: base Tool, schema generation
  via zod-to-json-schema. Then a first concrete tool (e.g. terminal) end-to-end.
- **P4 — LLM layer.** Biggest risk. LiteLLM has no TS equivalent — decide adapter strategy
  (call a provider lib directly? thin HTTP? reuse agent-server?). Provider abstraction,
  message formatting, prompt caching, routing. This phase likely splits into sub-beads.
- **P5 — Conversation + agent loop.** LocalConversation, RemoteConversation, ConversationState,
  the agent step loop, stuck detection. Depends on event + llm + tool.
- **P6 — Context & condenser, skills.** Context window management, condensation, skill
  discovery/validation.
- **P7 — Security, hooks, critic, subagent, git, mcp.** The surrounding subsystems.
- **P8 — Concrete tools (`openhands-tools` equivalent).** Terminal, file editor, browser,
  grep/glob, task tracker, etc. May be a separate package `@smolpaws/openhands-tools`.
- **P9 — Packaging, examples, docs, release 0.1.0.**

## Open decisions (resolve early, track as sub-beads)

1. **zod v3 vs v4** — default v3 for oh-tab parity. CONFIRM with Engel.
2. **Monorepo vs single package** — Python is a uv workspace (sdk/tools/workspace/server).
   Do we mirror with npm workspaces (`@smolpaws/openhands-agent`, `-tools`, `-server`)?
   Recommendation: start single-package, split when `tools`/`server` land.
3. **LLM provider strategy** — no LiteLLM in TS. Options: (a) direct provider SDKs behind an
   abstraction, (b) thin OpenAI-compatible HTTP client, (c) defer to agent-server. Needs a
   spike.
4. **Async model** — Python uses asyncio; TS uses native promises/async. Map generators/
   async-iterators for streaming.
5. **How closely to track upstream** — Python SDK is moving (68 commits in the last ~month).
   Pick a pinned upstream commit as the transpilation target and record it; catch up in
   batches rather than chasing HEAD.

## Reference materials

- Python source: `~/repos/agent-sdk/openhands-sdk/openhands/sdk` and `openhands-tools`
- Old TS attempt (reference only, do not copy): `~/repos/oh-tab/packages/agent-sdk`
  (240 files, ~39k LOC, ~v0.10.0 — useful for tooling, test shapes, zod patterns)
- Wire protocol: agent-server API + `OpenHands/typescript-client`

## Definition of done for THIS bead

A reviewed, agreed roadmap. Then spawn the P1…P9 beads with dependencies and start P1.
notes:
# Decisions resolved (2026-06-23, with Engel)

1. **zod version → v4** (4.4.3 is npm `latest`). Greenfield, no migration cost; ~2-3x faster
   parse + faster tsc at our schema volume; native `z.toJSONSchema()` lets us DROP the separate
   `zod-to-json-schema` dependency. DONE: package.json now uses zod ^4.4.3, no zod-to-json-schema.

2. **Packaging → single package for starters.** Split into npm workspaces (-tools, -server)
   later if/when those land.

3. **LLM layer → thin abstraction, 4 fat clients.** `LLMClient` is a DELIBERATELY THIN
   interface. Most logic lives INSIDE each client. We do NOT over-abstract — no leaky common
   layer trying to unify request/response shapes. We focus one client at a time and do the
   right thing for the performance + correctness of THAT specific API.
   The 4 clients:
   - OpenAI / OpenAI-compatible (chat completions)
   - Anthropic Messages
   - Gemini (new interactions API)
   - OpenAI Responses API
   Each owns its own request building, streaming, prompt caching, error mapping — whatever that
   API needs done correctly. The shared abstraction is only the minimal surface the
   conversation/agent loop calls.
   Live testing: in-repo scripts under `scripts/live/` (NOT CI) that hit each real API on demand.
   Keys come from a GitHub **environment** named `llm`. We run them occasionally to confirm each
   API still works. P4 structure: one sub-bead per client (done properly, end-to-end) + the
   live-test harness + the minimal shared interface — built last, extracted from what the
   clients actually share, not designed up front.

4. **Upstream pin → Python `OpenHands/software-agent-sdk` main @**
   `966340979be26c2162e9ab8805557b715e1f1a78` (2026-06-23, "Fix PR template HTML comment syntax
   #3862"). This is the transpilation target commit. Catch up to newer upstream in deliberate
   batches, not by chasing HEAD. DONE: local ~/repos/agent-sdk synced to this exact commit.

title: P5 — Conversation + agent loop
description: Transpile LocalConversation, RemoteConversation, ConversationState, the agent step loop, and stuck detection. Parent: openhands-agent-jad.

title: P1 — Foundations: utils, logger, io, event model
description: Transpile the low-dependency leaf modules first: utils, logger, io, and the event model hierarchy. Establishes the zod v4 patterns (pydantic BaseModel -> zod schema + z.infer) and the event discriminated-union shape that conversation/agent build on. Include serialization round-trip tests against Python-produced JSON fixtures. Parent plan: openhands-agent-jad.
